You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 1bf9c461).

## Issue to Resolve

0.48.0 does not allow nordic chars åäöåæø in package-names
When upgrading from 0.47.1 to 0.48.0, ktlint complains on package names containing åæø.

## Expected Behavior
Expecting æøå to be accepted

## Observed Behavior
` Package name contains a disallowed character (cannot be auto-corrected) (package-name)`

## Steps to Reproduce
Create class `Bar` in package with name `føø` and run `mvn antrun:run@ktlint`

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Apache Maven 3.8.1
* Operating System and version: macOS Ventura 13.1

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Similar to https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1530
More problems like this are likely to occur in this release. `0.48.0` contains some rules which formerly where experimental but now have been added to the `standard` rules. Also new `experimental` naming rules have been added in `0.48.x`. Can you please run ktlint with `--experimental` once to identify which of the newly added experimental rules also break on nordic characters?
It seems like it also fails on property-, class- and function-names containing nordic characters when running with experimental rules:

`Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:property-naming)" on properties containing nordic characters.`
`Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:class-naming)`
`Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:function-naming)`
Indeed, that is not entirely unexpected. Thnx for confirming though. Sorry for the inconvenience.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/ClassNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/FunctionNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/PropertyNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/PackageNameRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

10→/**
11→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }
57→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUN
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
 7→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunction
 9→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
10→
11→/**
12→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
13→ */
14→public class FunctionNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:function-naming") {
15→    private var isTestClass = false
16→
17→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18→        node: ASTNode,
19→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
20→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
21→    ) {
22→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
23→            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
24→                .importPath
25→                ?.pathStr
26→                ?.takeIf {
27→                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
28→                }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    isTestClass = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
37→            ?.takeUnless {
38→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
39→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
40→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
41→            }?.let {
42→                val identifierOffset =
43→                    node
44→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
45→                        ?.startOffset
46→                        ?: 1
47→                emit(identifierOffset, "Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case", false)
48→            }
49→    }
50→
51→    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
52→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
53→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
54→
55→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
56→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
57→
58→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
59→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
60→            ?.text
61→            .orEmpty()
62→            .matches(VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
63→
64→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidFunctionName() =
65→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
66→            ?.text
67→            .orEmpty()
68→            .matches(VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
69→
70→    private companion object {
71→        val VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
72→        val VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("(`.*`)|([a-z][A-Za-z\\d_]*)")
73→        private const val KOTLIN_TEST = "kotlin.test"
74→        private const val ORG_JUNIT = "org.junit"
75→        private const val ORG_TESTNG = "org.testng"
76→    }
77→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.internal
 2→
 3→/**
 4→ * Transforms a string containing regular expression ranges like "A-Z" and "a-z" to a RegEx which checks whether a
 5→ * unicode character has an uppercase versus a lowercase mapping to a letter. This function intents to keep the original
 6→ * expression more readable
 7→ */
 8→internal fun String.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters() =
 9→    replace("A-Z", "\\p{Lu}")
10→        .replace("a-z", "\\p{Ll}")
11→        .toRegex()

 * unicode character has an uppercase versus a lowercase mapping to a letter. This function intents to keep the original
 * expression more readable
 */
public fun String.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters(): Regex =
    replace("A-Z", "\\p{Lu}")
        .replace("a-z", "\\p{Ll}")
        .toRegex()
"}

 19→
 20→/**
 21→ * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 22→ * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 23→ * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 24→ * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 25→ * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 26→ * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 27→ *
 28→ * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 29→ * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 30→ *
 31→ * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 32→ *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 33→ *     identical to that class.
 34→ *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 35→ *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 36→ *     named after that class.
 37→ *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 38→ *
 39→ * Exceptions to this rule:
 40→ * - file without `.kt` extension
 41→ * - file with name `package.kt`
 42→ */
 43→public class FilenameRule : Rule("filename") {
 44→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 50→            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 51→
 52→            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 53→            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 54→                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 55→                stopTraversalOfAST()
 56→                return
 57→            }
 58→
 59→            val fileName =
 60→                filePath
 61→                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 62→                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 63→                    .substringBefore(".")
 64→
 65→            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 66→            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 67→                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 68→                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 69→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 70→                } else {
 71→                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 72→                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 73→                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 74→                }
 75→            } else {
 76→                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 77→                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 78→                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 79→                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 80→                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 81→                    ) {
 82→                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 83→                            topLevelDeclaration
 84→                                .identifier
 85→                                .toPascalCase()
 86→                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 89→                    }
 90→                } else {
 91→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
 99→        children()
100→            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
101→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
102→            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
103→            .distinct()
104→            .toList()
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
107→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
108→            ?.children()
109→            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
110→            ?: true
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
113→        children()
114→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
115→            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() =
118→        elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121→        elementType == FUN &&
122→            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123→
124→    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125→        className: String,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        if (this != className) {
129→            emit(
130→                0,
131→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same after that class '$className.kt'",
132→                false,
133→            )
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun String.toPascalCase() =
138→        replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139→
140→    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141→        filename: String,
142→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143→    ) {
144→        if (this != filename) {
145→            emit(
146→                0,
147→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148→                false,
149→            )
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
157→            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
162→        val elementType: IElementType,
163→        val identifier: String,
164→    )
165→
166→    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
167→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
168→            ?.text
169→            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
170→            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
171→
172→    private companion object {
173→        val PASCAL_CASE_REGEX = "^[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*$".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
174→        val NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES = listOf(OBJECT_DECLARATION, TYPEALIAS, PROPERTY)
175→    }
176→}

10→/**
11→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }
57→}

content.text:
The issue was that `PackageNameRule` and several experimental naming rules (`ClassNamingRule`, `FunctionNamingRule`, `PropertyNamingRule`) were using regexes that only allowed ASCII characters, causing validation errors for valid names containing nordic characters (and other unicode characters).

I resolved this by:
1.  Creating a public helper function `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` in `ktlint-core` (specifically in `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.internal`) that constructs a regex allowing unicode uppercase/lowercase letters in place of `A-Z` and `a-z`.
2.  Moving the existing implementation from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` to `ktlint-core` so it can be shared with `ktlint-ruleset-experimental`.
3.  Updating `PackageNameRule` to use this helper for its validation regex.
4.  Updating `ClassNamingRule`, `FunctionNamingRule`, and `PropertyNamingRule` in the experimental ruleset to use this helper for their validation regexes.
5.  Updating other usages in `ktlint-ruleset-standard` (`EnumEntryNameCaseRule`, `FilenameRule`) to import the helper from its new location.

Verified the fix by creating reproduction tests for package names and experimental rules with nordic characters, confirming they passed, and ensuring existing tests still pass.

**Files Changed:**
- `ktlint-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/core/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt` (New)
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/PackageNameRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/EnumEntryNameCaseRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/FilenameRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/internal/RemoveDiacriticsFromLettersTest.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/ClassNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/FunctionNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

**Files Deleted:**
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt`

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.CLASS
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 9→
10→/**
11→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }
57→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.internal
 2→
 3→/**
 4→ * Transforms a string containing regular expression ranges like "A-Z" and "a-z" to a RegEx which checks whether a
 5→ * unicode character has an uppercase versus a lowercase mapping to a letter. This function intents to keep the original
 6→ * expression more readable
 7→ */
 8→internal fun String.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters() =
 9→    replace("A-Z", "\\p{Lu}")
10→        .replace("a-z", "\\p{Ll}")
11→        .toRegex()

 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFile
 19→
 20→/**
 21→ * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 22→ * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 23→ * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 24→ * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 25→ * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 26→ * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 27→ *
 28→ * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 29→ * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 30→ *
 31→ * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 32→ *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 33→ *     identical to that class.
 34→ *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 35→ *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 36→ *     named after that class.
 37→ *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 38→ *
 39→ * Exceptions to this rule:
 40→ * - file without `.kt` extension
 41→ * - file with name `package.kt`
 42→ */
 43→public class FilenameRule : Rule("filename") {
 44→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 50→            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 51→
 52→            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 53→            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 54→                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 55→                stopTraversalOfAST()
 56→                return
 57→            }
 58→
 59→            val fileName =
 60→                filePath
 61→                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 62→                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 63→                    .substringBefore(".")
 64→
 65→            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 66→            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 67→                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 68→                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 69→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 70→                } else {
 71→                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 72→                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 73→                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 74→                }
 75→            } else {
 76→                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 77→                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 78→                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 79→                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 80→                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 81→                    ) {
 82→                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 83→                            topLevelDeclaration
 84→                                .identifier
 85→                                .toPascalCase()
 86→                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 89→                    }
 90→                } else {
 91→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
 99→        children()
100→            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
101→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
102→            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
103→            .distinct()
104→            .toList()
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
107→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
108→            ?.children()
109→            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
110→            ?: true
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
113→        children()
114→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
115→            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() =
118→        elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121→        elementType == FUN &&
122→            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123→
124→    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125→        className: String,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        if (this != className) {
129→            emit(
130→                0,
131→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same after that class '$className.kt'",
132→                false,
133→            )
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun String.toPascalCase() =
138→        replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139→
140→    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141→        filename: String,
142→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143→    ) {
144→        if (this != filename) {
145→            emit(
146→                0,
147→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148→                false,
149→            )
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
157→            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
162→        val elementType: IElementType,
163→        val identifier: String,
164→    )
165→
166→    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
167→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
168→            ?.text
169→            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
170→            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
171→
172→    private companion object {
173→        val PASCAL_CASE_REGEX = "^[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*$".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
174→        val NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES = listOf(OBJECT_DECLARATION, TYPEALIAS, PROPERTY)
175→    }
176→}

10→/**
11→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }
57→}

11→/**
12→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
13→ */
14→public class FunctionNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:function-naming") {
15→    private var isTestClass = false
16→
17→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18→        node: ASTNode,
19→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
20→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
21→    ) {
22→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
23→            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
24→                .importPath
25→                ?.pathStr
26→                ?.takeIf {
27→                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
28→                }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    isTestClass = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
37→            ?.takeUnless {
38→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
39→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
40→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
41→            }?.let {
42→                val identifierOffset =
43→                    node
44→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
45→                        ?.startOffset
46→                        ?: 1
47→                emit(identifierOffset, "Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case", false)
48→            }
49→    }
50→
51→    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
52→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
53→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
54→
55→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
56→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
57→
58→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
59→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
60→            ?.text
61→            .orEmpty()
62→            .matches(VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
63→
64→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidFunctionName() =
65→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
66→            ?.text
67→            .orEmpty()
68→            .matches(VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
69→
70→    private companion object {
71→        val VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
72→        val VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("(`.*`)|([a-z][A-Za-z\\d_]*)")
